item.title: Funding
item.description: ERC CNRS LCPQ FERMI NEXT NanoX UPS

item.title: People
item.description: Group Members Pierre-Francois Loos (group leader, CV) Follow @TitouLoos Directeur de recherche (HDR) Laboratoire de Chimie et Physique Quantiques Université de Toulouse, CNRS, UPS, France Researcher ID: A-1103-2010 ORCID: 0000-0003-0598-7425 Telephone: +33 5 6155 7339 E-mail: loos@irsamc.ups-tlse.fr Marios-Petros Kitsaras (Postdoc) Mauricio Rodríguez-Mayorga (Postdoc, NanoX) Loris Burth (PhD student) Lilian Cabirol (M2 student) Collaborators Johannes Tolle Junior Research Group Leader (University of Hamburg, Hamburg, Germany) Carlos Mejuto-Zaera CNRS researcher (Laboratoire de Physique Théorique, Toulouse, France)

item.title: Publications
item.description: 2026 162. An algebraic-diagrammatic construction for vertex corrections to the $GW$ self-energy, A. Marie, J. Tölle, and P.-F. Loos, J. Phys. Chem. Lett. (submitted). 161. From full dynamic to pure static: A family of $GW$-based approximations, P.-F. Loos and J. Tölle, J. Chem. Phys. (submitted). 160. Reference energies for non-relativistic core ionization potentials, A. Marie, L. Burth, and P.-F. Loos, J. Chem. Theory Comput. (submitted). 159. Connections between $GW$ and extended coupled cluster,

item.title: Research
item.description: Research interest I am currently a full-time CNRS senior researcher at the Universite Paul Sabatier (Toulouse, France). I am part of the Laboratoire de Chimie et Physique Quantiques (UMR5626) within the THEO group. ERC Consolidator grant (No. 863481): Processes related to electronically excited states are central in chemistry, physics, and biology, playing a key role in ubiquitous processes such as photochemistry, catalysis, and solar cell technology. However, defining an effective method that reliably provides accurate excited-state energies remains a major challenge in theoretical chemistry.

item.title: Talks
item.description: Oral communications 80. Falling into the Black Hole of Vertex Corrections, Marcus Center Workshops on Electronic Structure Theory, Caltech, Los Angeles, US (June 2026). 79. An algebraic-diagrammatic construction scheme for vertex corrections to the GW self-energy, ICQC 2026, UC Berkeley, San Francisco, US (June 2026). 78. Highly Accurate Excited-State Energies and Properties: The QUEST database, Hamburg University, Hamburg, Germany (May 2026). 77. Accurate trial wave functions from selected CI for ground and excited states,
